channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill at least 4 people and wound 16
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian missile and drone attacks on Ukraine overnight into Saturday killed at least four people and wounded 16 others, local officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Thailand's Queen Mother Sirikit has died at age 93
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand’s Queen Mother Sirikit, who supervised royal projects to help the rural poor, preserve traditional craft-making and protect the environment, died on Friday. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: DENIS D. GRAY, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and TIAN MACLEOD JI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Photos of Melodee Buzzard 'look like her': Aunt
item.description: Melodee's aunt, Lizabeth Meza, joined "Banfield" on Friday night and said she had not seen Melodee for over four years.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-abortion pregnancy centers are looking to offer much more than ultrasounds and diapers
item.description: More anti-abortion pregnancy centers have been offering medical services such as testing for sexually transmitted infections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump ends trade talks with Canada over tariffs ad that Ontario premier now says he'll phase out
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced he’s ending “all trade negotiations” with Canada because of a television ad sponsored by one of its provinces that used the words of former President Ronald Reagan to criticize U.S. tariffs — prompting the province's leader to later pull the ad.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, SEUNG MIN KIM and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials investigating after deadly crane collapse in commercial area north of Boston
item.description: Authorities, including officials from OSHA, are investigating after a fatal crane collapse at a commercial site north of Boston in Everett, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill to fund SNAP in shutdown introduced in House
item.description: Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa) on Friday introduced a bill to fund the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as the government shutdown threatens payments of the food aid in some states.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KREX 5

item.title: Rosa Parks and Helen Keller statues unveiled at the Alabama Capitol
item.description: Statues of Rosa Parks and Helen Keller, pivotal figures who fought for justice and inspired change across the world, were unveiled Friday on the grounds of the Alabama Capitol.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 7 charged in 2024 Pennsylvania voter registration fraud that prosecutors say was motivated by money
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A yearlong investigation into suspected fraudulent voter registration forms submitted ahead of last year's presidential election produced criminal charges Friday against six street canvassers and the man who led their work in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOJ prepares to send election monitors to California, New Jersey following requests from state GOPs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Department of Justice is preparing to send federal election observers to California and New Jersey next month, targeting two Democratic states holding off-year elections following requests from state Republican parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Reagan ad that infuriated Trump set to run during World Series
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said on Friday that the anti-tariffs advertisement featuring former President Reagan, which angered President Trump, will air during the World Series before being paused on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, WGN 9

item.title: US says it now plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia as soon as Oct. 31
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The U.S. government plans to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, and could do so as early as Oct. 31, according to a Friday court filing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pentagon accepts $130 million donation to help pay the military during the government shutdown
item.description: The Pentagon confirmed Friday that it has accepted an anonymous $130 million gift to help pay members of the military during the government shutdown, raising ethical questions after President Donald Trump had announced that a friend had offered the gift to defray any shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mafia returns to center stage in NBA betting scandal that included rigged poker games
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Decades after a crackdown by prosecutors decimated the ranks of the New York Mafia, the indictment of an NBA coach, a player and nearly three dozen others in a betting scandal highlighted the mob's persistence and adaptability to changing times and technology.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Urinary tract infections linked to contaminated food
item.description: Almost 1 in 5 urinary tract infections are connected to contaminated meat due to E. coli strains, according to a study.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: Over 141,000 bottles of cholesterol medication have been recalled due to “failed dissolution specifications,” suggesting that the drugs did not pass a solubility test, or failed to dissolve at a standard rate.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Dashcam video shows deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant trucker
item.description: Federal officials warned California about truck license compliance issues before deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant driver killed three.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palisades Fire suspect has courtroom outburst while pleading not guilty at arraignment
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht, 29, who is facing charges for allegedly starting what became the devastating Palisades Fire, reportedly had a courtroom outburst.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Homeowner arrested after armed clash with teen prankster: report
item.description: Teen alleges Florida man forced him to knees at gunpoint over doorbell prank. Conflicting stories emerge as homeowner faces assault charges in Boca Raton incident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hollywood piano teacher allegedly flees country after sexual abuse conviction involving teen
item.description: Hollywood piano teacher John Kaleel convicted of sexually abusing teen client allegedly fled country before sentencing, facing up to 10 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PHOTOS: Thieves cut through roof, steal hundreds of shoes in Florida mall heist
item.description: Florida authorities are searching for suspects who investigators said pulled off an elaborate heist to steal thousands of dollars in shoes from a Martin County mall this week.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KRON4

item.title: Here's what the White House lost with the East Wing's demolition
item.description: The East Wing's razing was met with criticism both by preservationists and by over half of the American public.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eric Adams endorses once bitter rival Andrew Cuomo in New York City's mayoral race
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed Andrew Cuomo in the city’s mayoral election Thursday, throwing his weight behind a bitter rival as he cast the Democratic nominee, Zohran Mamdani, as an avatar for radicals and gentrifiers.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deadly semitrailer crash in California renews federal criticism of immigrant truck drivers
item.description: A 21-year-old semitruck driver accused of being under the influence of drugs and causing a fiery crash that killed three people on a southern California freeway is in the country illegally, U.S. Homeland Security officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: States worry about how to fill the gap in food aid ahead of a federal benefits halt
item.description: Officials in Louisiana, Vermont and Virginia pledged Thursday to keep food aid flowing to recipients in their states, even if the federal program is stalled next month because of the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois gubernatorial candidate's son, daughter-in-law and grandchildren killed in helicopter crash
item.description: Darren Bailey, a former state senator, officially kicked off his campaign last month to regain the Republican nomination for governor after losing to Democratic Gov. JB Pritzker in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, Fox 5

item.title: How Trump and Xi are doing a delicate, sometimes pointed dance in bilateral trade talks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The calm following the Sept. 19 phone call between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping had barely ended when strong undercurrents surged, threatening to rock the entire ship. First, the U.S. government widened sanction rules on Chinese companies.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SNAP benefits may run out Nov. 1. Here's how you could still get food assistance
item.description: A disruption or delay could impact 42 million people, or 1 in 8 Americans, who rely on SNAP to buy food.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: 4 family members of Illinois governor candidate killed in Montana helicopter crash, campaign says
item.description: Four family members of a Republican running for governor in Illinois were killed in a Montana helicopter crash, his campaign said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Backing Israel was considered mandatory for New York politicians. Then came Zohran Mamdani
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A few weeks before his stunning loss to Zohran Mamdani in the Democratic mayoral primary, former Gov. Andrew Cuomo put forth a political calculus long accepted as fact in New York: “Being a Democrat,” he said, “it’s synonymous that you support Israel.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: How inflatable costumes ballooned at anti-Trump rallies due to a protester's frog outfit
item.description: Puffed-out costumes — hippos in tutus, Mr. Potato Heads, dinosaurs — have quickly become a feature of protests against President Donald Trump’s administration, including the massive "No Kings" marches across the U.S. last weekend.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump says he's owed a 'lot of money' over federal probes. Here's how the government could pay him
item.description: The potential that the president might take taxpayer money from the same government he leads has raised numerous ethical questions, especially since Trump has made cutting federal spending a top administration priority.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Broadway musicians reach tentative labor deal, averting a strike
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The union representing Broadway's musicians reached a tentative labor agreement with commercial producers on Thursday, averting a potentially crippling strike that would have silenced nearly two dozen musicals.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mamdani, Cuomo and Sliwa clash bitterly during final NYC mayoral debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani was attacked over his thin resume, Republican Curtis Sliwa strove to prove his seriousness as a candidate and former Gov.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NTSB releases report on plane crash involving Grammy Award-winning songwriter
item.description: MACON COUNTY, N.C. (WSPA) -- The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) has released a preliminary report on the fatal crash, which involved an aircraft registered to Grammy Award-winning songwriter Brett James.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, WGN 9
